closurec
Attempts to compile the given source, first as an expression and
       then as a statement if the first approach fails.
       Utility function to accept strings in functions that otherwise
       expect code objects
    
eval
exec)

source
name
/usr/lib64/python3.9/dis.py
_try_compile
file
depthc
Disassemble classes, methods, functions, and other compiled objects.
    With no argument, disassemble the last traceback.
    Compiled objects currently include generator objects, async generator
    objects, and coroutine objects, all of which store their code object
    in a special attribute.
    N

z1Formatted details of methods, functions, or code.)

Name:              %sz

Argument count:    %sz

z}Print details of methods, functions, or code to *file*.
    If *file* is not provided, the output is printed on stdout.
    r%
_InstructionzBopname opcode arg argval argrepr offset starts_line is_jump_targetz!Human readable name for operationz
Numeric code for operationz6Numeric argument to operation (if any), otherwise Nonez4Resolved arg value (if known), otherwise same as argz0Human readable description of operation argumentz1Start index of operation within bytecode sequencez4Line started by this opcode (if any), otherwise Nonez1True if other code jumps to here, otherwise False
Details for a bytecode operation
       Defined fields:
         opname - human readable name for operation
         opcode - numeric code for operation
         arg - numeric argument to operation (if any), otherwise None
         argval - resolved arg value (if known), otherwise same as arg
         argrepr - human readable description of operation argument
         offset - start index of operation within bytecode sequence
         starts_line - line started by this opcode (if any), otherwise None
         is_jump_target - True if other code jumps to here, otherwise False
    
Format instruction details for inclusion in disassembly output
        *lineno_width* sets the width of the line number field (0 omits it)
        *mark_as_current* inserts a '-->' marker arrow as part of the line
        *offset_width* sets the width of the instruction offset field
        Nz
%%%dd
-->z
   z

is_jump_targetr

_disassemble
Instruction._disassembleN)
__module__
__qualname__
__doc__r
first_linec
Iterator for the opcodes in methods, functions or code
    Generates a series of Instruction named tuples giving the details of
    each operations in the supplied code.
    If *first_line* is not None, it indicates the line number that should
    be reported for the first source line in the disassembled code.
    Otherwise, the source line information (if any) is taken directly from
    the disassembled code object.
    Nr
dictr	
co_firstlineno
_get_instructions_bytesr,
cell_names
linestarts
line_offsetr
Helper to get optional details about const references
       Returns the dereferenced constant and its repr if the constant
       list is defined.
       Otherwise returns the constant index and its repr().
    N
const_indexZ
const_list
argvalr
_get_const_info
Helper to get optional details about named references
       Returns the dereferenced name as both value and repr if the name
       list is defined.
       Otherwise returns the name index and its repr().
    Nr
name_indexZ	name_listr
_get_name_info'
Iterate over the instructions in a bytecode string.
    Generates a sequence of Instruction namedtuples giving the details of each
    opcode.  Additional information about the code's runtime environment
    (e.g. variable names, constants) can be specified using optional
    arguments.
    Nr

Find the offsets in a byte code which are start of lines in the source.
    Generate pairs (offset, lineno) as described in Python/compile.c.

The bytecode operations of a piece of code
    Instantiate this with a function, method, other compiled object, string of
    code, or a code object (as returned by compile()).
    Iterating over this yields the bytecode operations as Instruction instances.
    N)
